exercises.title: A VerificationGate protocol
exercises.prompt: A verification gate decides whether a tool call is allowed to fire via a deterministic `evaluate(call)` returning allow/deny with a reason. Build the gate protocol and two concrete gates (a whitelist gate and a regex gate), and show each allowing a permitted call and denying a disallowed one. Emphasize determinism (same input -> same verdict).
exercises.hint: Gate protocol: evaluate(call) -> {'allow': bool, 'reason': str}, deterministic. WhitelistGate: allow if tool in allowed set. RegexGate: deny if args match a forbidden pattern. Show each gate's allow/deny. Deterministic = auditable.

tests.desc: clean args pass the regex gate

exercises.title: Compose gates into a short-circuit chain
exercises.prompt: Gates compose into a chain with short-circuit semantics: the first gate to deny stops the chain (the call never fires). Build a `GateChain` that runs gates in order and returns the first denial (or allow if all pass), and show a call denied by the second gate without the third running. Discuss why short-circuit ordering (cheap gates first) matters.
exercises.hint: Chain runs gates in order; first deny short-circuits (later gates skipped). Show a call passing gate 1, denied by gate 2, gate 3 never evaluated. Order cheap/common-deny gates first to short-circuit early (fail fast).

tests.desc: the first deny names its index

tests.expr: (lambda ev: (GateChain([lambda c: {'allow':False,'reason':'x'}, lambda c: ev.append('ran') or {'allow':True,'reason':'y'}]).evaluate({}), ev)[1])([]) == []

exercises.title: The observation ledger and budget gate
exercises.prompt: The observation budget caps how many tokens the model is shown — an observation ledger tracks every token, and a budget gate stops the loop when the agent has read too much (context poisoning / runaway cost). Build the ledger + a budget gate that truncates oversized tool output and halts the loop when the cumulative observation budget is exhausted. Show output truncated and the loop halting on budget exhaustion.
exercises.hint: Ledger tracks cumulative observed tokens. Budget gate: truncate a single output to a per-call cap; halt the loop when cumulative exceeds the total budget. Show a big output truncated and the loop halting after enough observations. Why: too much context poisons reasoning and runs up cost.
exercises.solution:
The observation budget bounds *how much the model reads* — a ledger tracks every token shown, and the budget gate truncates and halts.

```python
class ObservationLedger:
    def __init__(self, total_budget, per_call_cap):
        self.total_budget = total_budget; self.per_call_cap = per_call_cap; self.observed = 0
    def admit(self, output_tokens):
        if self.observed >= self.total_budget:
            return None, 'HALT: observation budget exhausted'
        shown = min(output_tokens, self.per_call_cap)        # truncate oversized output
        self.observed += shown
        truncated = shown < output_tokens
        return shown, f"observed {shown} tokens{' (truncated)' if truncated else ''}; total {self.observed}"

ledger = ObservationLedger(total_budget=1000, per_call_cap=400)
for out in (200, 5000, 500, 500):     # second is huge -> truncated; later ones exhaust budget
    shown, msg = ledger.admit(out)
    print(f"tool output {out:5d} tokens -> {msg}")
    if shown is None: break
```

Output:

```
tool output   200 tokens -> observed 200 tokens; total 200
tool output  5000 tokens -> observed 400 tokens (truncated); total 600
tool output   500 tokens -> observed 400 tokens (truncated); total 1000
tool output   500 tokens -> HALT: observation budget exhausted
```

**Why an observation budget.** Two failure modes motivate it: *context poisoning* (a giant tool output floods the context with noise, degrading the model's reasoning) and *runaway cost* (every observed token is a token the model re-reads on every subsequent turn — the quadratic context cost from Capstone 01). The *per-call cap* truncates a single oversized output (the 5000-token output is cut to 400); the *total budget* halts the loop when cumulative observations exhaust it. The ledger makes the budget *enforceable* by tracking every token the model has been shown.

**The verification synthesis.** Deterministic gates (Ex. 1) compose into a short-circuiting chain (Ex. 2), and an observation ledger plus budget gate bound how much the model reads (Ex. 3). Together they are the deterministic layer that decides *whether* a call fires, *how much* of its output the agent sees, and *when* the loop must stop. This is the verification layer without which the harness is "a wish in a trenchcoat" — and it pairs with the sandbox (Lesson 26) that decides what happens when a call actually runs.

tests.desc: output within the cap is admitted
oversized output is truncated to the cap
